threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to the Clapton Pond as a park, not realising it’s more like a glorified puddle where the locals go to watch ducks bicker.
Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading the night bus crew with his guitar at Brixton. Half the passengers joined in, a real community vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a mom on Karl-Marx-Straße bargaining with a guy selling churros while her kid danced to the street musician's guitar. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves beer in bath tubs. Sounds odd, but it's a whole vibe after a long day!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they’ve witnessed a full BART evacuation and just shrug it off as another Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 5 taking a nap while holding a half-eaten burrito. Know you’re in the TL when lunch and rest are one and the same.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the local bars are all about tapas. But the real charm? The hidden vermuterias where the regulars sip on homemade vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they try to pay with a card at a cash-only churro stand. That look of confusion is priceless.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Underground' instead of just 'the Tube.' It’s like a first date mistake here.
Heard a couple arguing on the 35 last night; she was convinced the bus was taking them 'the long way round' to Clapham, while he was just trying to read his paper in peace.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when I saw a guy juggling at Hermannplatz while a cat sat next to him, completely unfazed by the chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you see whole streets turn into open-air galleries during gallery weekends—artists painting live while you sip on coffee from a stand.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to carry an extra pair of shoes if they're taking BART after 5. Nothing ruins a Friday evening like overheated brakes and a forgotten backup plan.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a suit serenading the bus stop with an old Motown tune. The guitar? A broken broomstick. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they’re avoiding the crowds by coming to Gràcia, but they end up clumping in the same plazas like it’s a tourist trap. It’s wild.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewildered look when the tram suddenly stops, waiting for the traffic light to change – it’s like a rite of passage here.
